highlights.item: Homepage seed content now drives a real exterior-cleaning marketing surface instead of the Payload template fallback.
Instant quote UX is live on the homepage and posts usable lead data into Payload form submissions.
Customer-facing privacy, terms, refund, and contact-SLA pages are seeded as Payload `pages` content and linked from the public footer/contact flow.
The homepage renders seeded Payload layout blocks directly, and the reusable `serviceGrid` block now supports media, highlights, and pricing cues for image-led service cards.
Seed media now includes exterior-cleaning placeholder imagery with captions, the about page uses a stronger visual hero, and the shared header/footer no longer show the template Payload logo.
The `/contact` route now uses a compact icon-led tab row with a single detail panel for guidance, a higher-contrast shell, and a stronger request card border so the form reads as the primary action.

Public mobile QA now includes a compact drawer nav, tighter hero and service spacing, overflow guards on the frontend shell, and direct browser verification that home and contact hold their layout at 390px width without horizontal scroll.

The contact request form now includes topic, reply preference, and property address fields so support requests route cleanly while compact guidance tabs keep long policy detail out of the main mobile flow.

Testimonials are managed through a dedicated `testimonials` collection and optional Testimonials page block, and admin media generation is available directly from the `media` create view through the prompt-to-media sidebar control.
The image-generation workflow is now documented and executable through the repo MCP/media tooling, including `npm run generate:marketing-images` and the shared prompt manifest.
The homepage no longer renders package-card pricing, and the seeded header now sends visitors straight to the instant quote estimator as the public pricing path.

next.item: Replace placeholder property photos with generated or real Grime Time imagery before public-launch positioning. Prompts, batches, and wiring live in `src/lib/media/image-generation-manifest.ts` and `npm run generate:marketing-images`.
Optionally add the Testimonials block to approved public pages once marketing wants live social proof; until then, published content can remain staged in admin.
Re-seed or edit the `contact` page in Payload if an older environment still carries legacy generic Form blocks instead of the first-party `contactRequest` block.
